Code fragment: public final TableField<AssessmentsRecord, Float> MINIMAL_SEVERENESS = createField(DSL.name("minimal_severeness"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.REAL.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The column 'minimal_severeness' uses REAL (floating-point) type which can cause precision loss when storing fixed-precision values like severity scores. Consider using DECIMAL or NUMERIC type instead to ensure accurate storage and comparison of severity values.

Code fragment: public final TableField<SensorsRecord, Integer> SENSOR_ID = createField(DSL.name("sensor_id"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a synthetic auto-increment primary key (SENSOR_ID) even though a natural key composed of (DEVICE, BODY_PART) could serve as the primary key, since a sensor is uniquely identified by its device and body part combination. Consider using a composite primary key on (DEVICE, BODY_PART) instead of the synthetic SENSOR_ID.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the DEVICES table, which is inefficient and can break if the schema changes. Replace with `select(DEVICES.DEVICE, DEVICES.DESCRIPTION)` to explicitly fetch only the needed columns.

The TASKS.TASK_END column appears to be NULLABLE (as evidenced by the null check on line 635), but the query condition `TASKS.TASK_END.le(end)` will exclude rows where TASK_END is NULL, potentially omitting valid ongoing tasks. Consider using `TASKS.TASK_END.isNull().or(TASKS.TASK_END.le(end))` to include tasks that haven't ended yet.
